steps.title: Find T in radians by adding
steps.content:
Since T is $\frac{7\pi}{12}$ radians greater than R:
$$T = R + \frac{7\pi}{12} = \frac{5\pi}{6} + \frac{7\pi}{12}$$
Convert to common denominator 12:
$$\frac{5\pi}{6} = \frac{10\pi}{12}$$
So:
$$T = \frac{10\pi}{12} + \frac{7\pi}{12} = \frac{17\pi}{12}$$

steps.title: Convert to degrees
steps.content:
Multiply by the conversion factor $\frac{180°}{\pi}$:
$$T = \frac{17\pi}{12} \cdot \frac{180°}{\pi} = \frac{17 \cdot 180°}{12} = \frac{3060°}{12} = 255°$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
